title: chenopodium berlandieri var. bushianum
intro: Chenopodium berlandieri var. bushianum, also known as bushy lamb's quarters, is a variety of the Chenopodium berlandieri plant, a type of plant in the amaranth family that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of bushy lamb's quarters include reducing fever, relieving coughs, and easing congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. Bushy lamb's quarters can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall respiratory health and well-being.
